h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's step-by-step labeling instructions.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Both lines — one object, one cart. Track card still under the tail.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner identifies a structural pattern in the force labels, recognizing that each interaction involves one external object acting on the cart.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Only the thing touching here pushes. Bench or track? Name the up arrow. Then both arrows again. Page turned; interaction stayed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor responds to the learner's hesitation by physically manipulating the cards and introducing a touch-based constraint.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Only the thing touching here pushes. Bench or track? Name the up arrow. Then both arrows again. Page turned; interaction stayed.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The touch constraint is a useful physical rule, though the tutor's guidance remains highly directed.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to trigger contingent tutor uptake.
